title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 389.614 - Warning signals for crossings presumed adequate — duty of reasonable care required of railroads
text:
389.614.  Warning signals for crossings presumed adequate — duty of reasonable care required of railroads. — Railroad warning devices which are installed or maintained by order or by rule of the division of motor carrier and railroad safety are presumed to be adequate and appropriate warning devices for the crossing.  All railroads shall continue to exercise reasonable care at railroad crossings for the safety of the members of the public using the crossing.
(L. 1992 S.B. 765 § 2) 
Division of motor carrier and railroad safety abolished, duties and functions transferred to highways and transportation commission and department of transportation, 226.008
